readers.thesis: Backup sovereignty exists only when a second operator restores a usable service from independent copies after the primary environment is unavailable.
observations: Earlier runs separate authority from provider execution.
The platform requires destructive proof rather than backup-count claims.
uncertainties: The complete historical transcript and private archive were unavailable.
No measured full-service restore has run.

background.experience: long-term digital preservation and integrity review

readers.thesis: A canonical backup is an evidence-bearing recovery set whose integrity, retention, and restore acceptance remain controlled independently of every storage provider.
observations: Replication does not protect against logically valid destructive changes.
Content-addressed encrypted snapshots and database-native recovery solve different data classes.
uncertainties: Actual data volume and restore bandwidth remain unmeasured.

agreements: A stored copy does not count as a successful backup until an isolated restore passes.
At least one recovery copy remains outside normal production write authority.
Database recovery and artifact recovery use separate correctness tests.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes recovery speed and operator sequence; Reader B prioritizes preservation integrity and retention proof.
Reader A accepts three copies as an initial gate; Reader B treats failure-domain independence as more important than copy count.
omissions_detected: No current storage-volume, change-rate, or restore-throughput benchmark exists.
No second-operator blank-machine recovery has run.
No complete private corpus was available.
revisions_after_discussion: Require three copies across at least two failure domains and two sites, with one offline or append-only copy.
Measure both integrity and time-to-usable-service in the same quarterly recovery exercise.

notes_added.statement: Count a backup only after an isolated restore reproduces declared data and service behavior.
Keep at least one recovery path outside production write authority and prove provider replacement during restoration.

notes_challenged.challenge: Disposable workers preserve build evidence, but platform continuity still fails if canonical state cannot be restored independently.

privacy.review: Contains only abstract capability conclusions, synthetic reader profiles, coverage boundaries, and non-identifying architecture findings. It excludes transcript excerpts, personal facts, health or animal details, credentials, locations, deleted text, and unique conversation fragments.
